metadata.preview:
import test from 'node:test'
import assert from 'node:assert/strict'
import { formatFirstName, formatLastName } from './src/name-formatters.mjs'

test('name formatters preserve behavior', () => {
  assert.equal(formatFirstName('  Ada   Lovelace  '), 'Ada Lovelace')
  assert.equal(formatLastName('  van   Rossum  '), 'van Rossum')
})

display.text:
import test from 'node:test'
import assert from 'node:assert/strict'
import { formatFirstName, formatLastName } from './src/name-formatters.mjs'

test('name formatters preserve behavior', () => {
  assert.equal(formatFirstName('  Ada   Lovelace  '), 'Ada Lovelace')
  assert.equal(formatLastName('  van   Rossum  '), 'van Rossum')
})

state.title: bench-targets/refactor/name-formatters.test.mjs

part.text: I see the duplicated logic: both `formatFirstName` and `formatLastName` use the same `value.trim().replace(/\s+/g, ' ')` pattern. I'll extract this into a shared `normalizeName` helper.
